adding-and-subtracting.label: Adding and subtracting
adding-and-subtracting.summary: Adding and subtracting in Calculated Columns and SQL Expressions

fill-blank-1.title: Add a Value to a Column
fill-blank-1.prompt: Suppose you want to show each student's grade level next year. Which SQL expression correctly adds 1 to the current grade_level?
fill-blank-1.hint: Focus on the missing SQL concept rather than the exact missing word.

help.concept: The blank should be filled with the SQL term that matches the job the statement is trying to perform.
help.hint_1: Think about what the missing part is supposed to do in the statement.
help.hint_2: Choose the SQL term that best completes the meaning of the statement.

fill-blank-1.template: SELECT name, grade_level ___ 1 AS next_year_grade FROM students;

fill-blank-2.title: Subtracting for Graduation Countdown
fill-blank-2.prompt: To find out how many years a student has until graduation (grade 12), which value should fill the blank?
fill-blank-2.hint: Focus on the missing SQL concept rather than the exact missing word.

help.concept: The blank should be filled with the SQL term that matches the job the statement is trying to perform.
help.hint_1: Think about what the missing part is supposed to do in the statement.
help.hint_2: Choose the SQL term that best completes the meaning of the statement.

fill-blank-2.template: SELECT name, 12 ___ grade_level AS years_left FROM students;

single-choice-1.title: Understanding SQL Expressions
single-choice-1.prompt: Which of the following best describes a calculated column in SQL?
single-choice-1.hint: Calculated columns use expressions to create new values.

help.concept: A calculated column is generated by applying an expression or operation to one or more existing columns in a SELECT statement.
help.hint_1: Look for the option that involves creating new values from existing data.
help.hint_2: Calculated columns are not just copies; they are the result of expressions.

options.a: A column that is copied directly from the table.
options.b: A column created by performing an operation on existing columns.
options.c: A column that always contains text values.
options.d: A column that cannot be renamed.

multi-choice-1.title: Valid Arithmetic Expressions in SQL
multi-choice-1.prompt: Which of the following are valid ways to use arithmetic in a SQL SELECT statement? (Choose all that apply.)
multi-choice-1.hint: All standard arithmetic operators can be used in SQL expressions.

help.concept: SQL supports addition (+), subtraction (-), multiplication (*), and division (/) in SELECT statements and WHERE clauses.
help.hint_1: Check if each example uses a valid arithmetic operator and syntax.
help.hint_2: All four statements use arithmetic correctly in SQL.

options.d: SELECT name, grade_level FROM students WHERE grade_level / 2 = 5;

code-input-1.title: Write a Query with Addition and Subtraction
code-input-1.prompt: Write a SQL query that shows each student's name, their current grade_level, and what their grade will be two years from now (call it future_grade).
code-input-1.hint: Focus on the concept being tested.

help.concept: Think about the role or idea being tested rather than repeating answer wording.
help.hint_1: Eliminate choices or interpretations that do not match the task.
help.hint_2: Choose the concept that best fits what the exercise is asking you to do.

sketch-1.title: Using Arithmetic in SQL SELECT
sketch-1.bodyMarkdown:
SQL allows you to perform arithmetic operations directly in your SELECT statements. For example, you can add or subtract numeric columns to create new calculated columns in your result set. This is useful for generating new values from existing data, such as adjusting grades or calculating totals.

Example:

```sql
SELECT name, grade_level + 1 AS next_year_grade
FROM students;
```

This query shows each student's name and what their grade level will be next year.

sketch-2.title: Subtracting Values in SQL
sketch-2.bodyMarkdown:
You can also subtract values in SQL expressions. For instance, if you want to see how many years until a student graduates (assuming graduation is at grade 12), you can subtract their current grade from 12.

Example:

```sql
SELECT name, 12 - grade_level AS years_until_graduation
FROM students;
```

This query calculates how many years each student has left until graduation.
